mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is pinned near zero, liquidity conditions stay easy and the equity discount rate remains compressed, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as long as inflation is not accelerating sharply.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward returns are negative in a majority of periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5% and CPIAUCSL inflation is not accelerating, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above 8.5% signals labor-market stress and weaker household income growth, which depresses near-term earnings expectations and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes with UNRATE above 8.5% produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the signal loses sign stability across two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized improvement in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates broadening payroll income and retail demand, which lifts revenue growth expectations and should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive joint changes in PAYEMS and RSAFS do not outperform either series alone, or if 60-day returns are not positive in the top tercile of combined demand growth.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly rise in INDPRO after prior weakness marks an industrial rebound that improves cyclical earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises by more than 0.5% month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if post-trigger 5-day returns are not positive more than 50% of the time, or if the average return after an INDPRO surge is not above the unconditional average by at least 25 bps.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST remains elevated while GDPC1 is expanding, the housing and consumption transmission supports risk appetite and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days; the signal fails if housing strength occurs during recessionary GDP contractions.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined HOUST-GDPC1 signal is not positive in expansion periods, or if its predictive accuracy collapses when GDPC1 growth turns negative.

mechanism_narrative: A higher FEDFUNDS rate during a tightening cycle raises discount rates and tightens financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days, especially when CPIAUCSL inflation is still elevated.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered tightening episodes yield positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations, or if the signal is not stronger when CPIAUCSL is above its recent trend.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS series signals payroll contraction and weaker future consumption, which reduces earnings expectations and causes negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls for two consecutive monthly readings
failure_condition: Reject if two-month payroll declines are followed by positive 5-day returns more often than negative returns, or if the signal has no edge relative to a random baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A broad demand rebound in RSAFS and HOUST that occurs after a prior slowdown marks a recovery in consumer spending and housing turnover, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if combined gains in RSAFS and HOUST do not lead to positive 20-day returns more often than negative returns, or if the effect disappears outside recovery phases.
